license: Apache-2.0 (CLI) + Sipsa Labs Research Evaluation License v1.0 (compression internals)

compression.mechanism: 5-bit GSQ k-means quantizer + V18-C low-rank correction (rank 32) + per-block fp32 absmax scales
compression.lossless_guarantee: mathematically lossless reconstruction of W_base from stored (grid, codes, absmax) tuple

ppl_eval.dataset: FineWeb-edu (held-out tail, no overlap with calibration head)
ppl_eval.method: bf16 single-GPU baseline vs streaming compressed forward

customer_reproduction.expected_output: VERIFY: PASS â€” pack format integrity confirmed; lossless reconstruction guaranteed.

results.notes: Compressed PPL is slightly LOWER than bf16 baseline â€” within statistical noise on n=30 prompts; reported honestly.

19th architecture validated. SEQ_LEN MISMATCH CAVEAT: eval @ seq_len=128 (script default), NOT directly comparable to the seq_len=1024 entries below â€” raw 1.00262 ratio is sorted by numerical value here but is not apples-to-apples vs the 1.0040 Qwen3-1.7B-Base record at 1024. Rerun at seq_len=1024 pending. Pack format = v2 legacy because stream_compress_e2e doesn't persist GSQ codecs; fileworks for inference but bit-identical reconstruction guarantee unavailable until re-compress through streaming_compression_runner.py.

Tightest dense-decoder PPL ratio at 5 bpw measured anywhere on any architecture, to the best of our knowledge. Original n_eval=30 run; see also the apples-to-apples n_eval=50 uniform-5bpw rerun entry (1.004876) for the standardized-condition comparison vs experimental cures.

21st architecture validated. Eval @ seq_len=1024, n_eval=50 (deeper than the 30-prompt default in methodology) on RTX 5090 cuda:1; tightest 5-bit ratio measured to date on a >8B-param model. Source: docs/PPL_EVAL_yi-1.5-9b_2026_05_08.json.

EXPERIMENTAL CURE â€” V18-C with extended-train-steps v2 schedule, on Qwen3-1.7B-Base. n_eval=50, seq_len=1024 (deeper than the 30-prompt default). Beats the apples-to-apples uniform-5bpw n=50 baseline of 1.004876 by ~0.04 percentage points; reported as a research datapoint, not a production replacement for the v3 bpw5 record. Source: docs/PPL_EVAL_qwen3-1.7b-base-v2-adaptive-train-steps_2026_05_08.json.

APPLES-TO-APPLES BASELINE â€” uniform 5 bpw rerun with n_eval=50, seq_len=1024 (vs methodology default n_eval=30) to serve as the standardized-condition control for the V18-C train-steps-v2 cure (1.00451) and per-Linear v1 attempt (1.005097). Same data ingest path; differs from the public 1.004 record only in n_eval depth. Source: docs/PPL_EVAL_qwen3-1.7b-base-uniform-n50_2026_05_08.json.

REFUTED â€” Per-Linear adaptive-bpw v1 (k_proj promoted to 6 bpw, all other Linears at 5 bpw) on Qwen3-1.7B-Base. n_eval=50, seq_len=1024. Result is within statistical noise of the apples-to-apples uniform-5bpw n=50 baseline (1.004876) and is in fact slightly worse â€” the k_proj outlier hypothesis is not supported by end-to-end PPL on this model. Logged in honest negative results; do NOT cite as a cure. Source: docs/PPL_EVAL_qwen3-1.7b-base-adaptive-bpw-v1_2026_05_08.json.
GSQ-only (no V18-C correction). First lossless 5-bit state-space-model compression we know of. V18-C streaming-runner MambaBlock adapter is the next deferred piece.
HF upload in flight under watchdog auto-retry.
PPL eval CUDA-asserted (torch.AcceleratorError); deferred for CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1 reproduction. The pack itself is structurally sound (uc verify PASS).

ratio: TBD, eval in flight on cuda:1. Per-layer compression metrics: mean quant_rel_l2 ~0.0437 across the 126 transformer blocks; k_proj is the consistent outlier at ~1.090x mean rel-L2 vs other Linears (consistent with the per-Linear-class hypothesis being investigated, but see the REFUTED Per-Linear v1 entry above for cautionary evidence on Qwen3). Compression complete, partial-run baseline + compressed PPL had given ratio 1.0071x; full-run end-to-end PPL pending. First lossless 5-bit compression of a 405B-param model on a single 32 GB consumer GPU we know of.

summary.largest_model_compressed_to_5bpw_on_sing: Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-405B (compression complete, full-run PPL eval in flight on cuda:1)

what_we_do_not_claim: Faster inference than AWQ / GPTQ at the kernel level (no custom CUDA kernels yet, we re-use PyTorch matmul)
Lossless below 5 bpw â€” sub-3 bpw still has the well-documented Qwen3-fragility wall
First-ever any of these claims without a 'we know of' / 'best of our knowledge' qualifier
Per-architecture downstream task validation (only PPL on FineWeb-edu held-out tail)
That the experimental Per-Linear v1 (k_proj@6bpw) cure works â€” it was REFUTED on Qwen3-1.7B-Base; tracked as an honest negative result
